title: Baseline A vs Baseline B: OCR Grounding Impact

per_case_results.notes: Model read Y/Δ labels correctly but inverted the meaning; stated KM1+KM2 delta / KM3 star (opposite of what labels show). New hard-failure.

Both A and B claim K1/K2 are separate contactor coils. Reality: K1/K2 are wire-numbers on CR coil's own leads. OCR present in B but didn't resolve structural misunderstanding. +13 improvement from A but still hard-failure.
Largest positive delta. OCR text helped clarify timing sequence logic.
Stable performance. OCR provided minimal additional value.
Steady improvement. OCR text supported better component identification.
Unexplained regression despite OCR present. Model may have over-indexed on OCR text and missed visual context.
New hard-failure. Invented 'Safety PLC' as a component. Real component likely has a simpler label. OCR did not prevent semantic hallucination.

Largest positive delta but remains hard-failure. A: fabricated 'XC00' (eliminated in B via OCR). B: OCR proxy returned 'XC90' (unverified on real print). Model repeated it. CAVEAT: proxy quality issue; production glm-ocr needs testing.
Regression. OCR may have introduced ambiguity.

evidence_grounding.note: OCR grounding decreased confidence that claims are evidence-supported; two new hard-failures are confident-wrong readings.

eliminated_by_ocr.truth: C.T. in print = current transformers (not control transformer)
eliminated_by_ocr.baseline_b_result: Prevented by OCR labels showing 'C.T. = current transformers'

eliminated_by_ocr.truth: not present on print; only XC45/46/47/60/25A/25B
eliminated_by_ocr.baseline_b_result: Prevented by OCR labels (though B introduced new error: XC90)

new_or_persisted_errors.mechanism: Model read Y/Δ labels correctly but inverted their meaning; confident-wrong semantic assignment, not OCR failure
Semantic hallucination; OCR did not prevent confident naming of non-existent component
Structural misunderstanding (claimed separate coils vs. wire-numbers on single coil); OCR text present but didn't resolve

error_propagation_caveat.status: OCR proxy returned unverified label; model repeated it
error_propagation_caveat.note: Cascade-vision-OCR proxy is NOT production glm-ocr. Whether production glm-ocr exhibits the same propagation pattern is unknown. Requires staging/compose retest against production glm-ocr.

decision.headline: OCR grounding does NOT reliably reduce hallucinations

mechanisms.description: OCR text anchors responses when model would otherwise invent (cases 05, 20). Real device fabrications eliminated.
OCR proxy returned unverified 'XC90' where none existed. Model believed and repeated it. Production glm-ocr retest needed.

Even with OCR text present, model inverts semantic meaning (case 03: Y/Δ labels read backward) or invents non-existent devices with confidence (cases 03, 17). Not OCR-fixable without semantic guardrails.

decision.recommendation: Build SMALLEST DETERMINISTIC evidence-binding guard that prevents translator from asserting specific identifiers (component names, connector part numbers) without visible legible support. Guard must be EXPERIMENTAL, not optimized against judge, not deployed to production.
caveats: Baseline B uses cascade-vision-OCR proxy, not production glm-ocr. Error propagation (case 20: XC90) may be proxy-quality artifact.
Corpus N=10 (1 question each). Hard-failure rate doubling (20% → 40%) is real on this set but low power. Need ≥25 cases for reliable trend.
Corpus expanding; these are initial-stage results.
